fresh_facts.claim: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) and its 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit, carrying approximately 3,500 sailors and Marines, entered US Central Command's area of operations on March 27, 2026.
USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group/Marine Expeditionary Unit deployed from San Diego on March 18, 2026, en route to relieve USS Tripoli in the Middle East.

Pentagon finalized plans as of March 29, 2026, for potential ground operations in Iran involving 3,000-4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division focused on regional capacity including the Strait of Hormuz and Kharg Island.
The United States has approximately 50,000 troops deployed in the Middle East region prior to recent reinforcements.
Thirteen US troops have been killed and 290 wounded since February 28, 2026 strikes.
Iran continues missile and drone attacks on Israel, US positions, and energy targets following US expanded strikes on approximately 9,000 Iranian targets including missile and naval infrastructure.

The United States and Iran exchanged competing ceasefire proposals on March 25, 2026, with the US offering a 15-point framework including sanctions relief, nuclear rollback, IAEA monitoring, missile limits, and Strait access.
Iran's counter-ceasefire proposal includes halting strikes on officials, war reparations demands, and retaining Hormuz control.
The Trump administration extended a pause on Iranian energy strikes by 10 days as of March 29, 2026.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, an IRGC veteran, was appointed as Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, signaling a shift of power to military-security structures.
Following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death, IRGC hardliners from the Iran-Iraq war era have come to dominate Iranian leadership and decision-making.
A nationwide internet blackout has been enforced in Iran as part of state control measures amid military strikes.
Thailand secured safe passage for its oil vessels through the Strait of Hormuz per AFP report on March 28, 2026.
US-Israeli strikes targeting nuclear, military, and industrial Iranian targets have degraded Iranian naval and missile capabilities.
Oil prices have elevated due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and proxy threats including Houthi missiles and drone strikes on Oman port.
European officials warned against promoting preferred Iranian leaders to avoid reputational risks, per statement on March 28, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: U.S. and Israeli air campaign has struck approximately 9,000 Iranian targets with operations nearing completion, while Pentagon finalizes plans for potential ground operations involving 3,000-4,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division; the military-dominated regime under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei continues rejecting ceasefire terms despite massive degradation of naval and missile capabilities and a nationwide internet blackout.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Pentagon finalized plans as of March 29, 2026, for potential ground operations in Iran involving 3,000-4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division focused on the Strait of Hormuz and Kharg Island, escalating military pressure beyond the air campaign.
